reasoning:
I have verified through search that global internet freedom, as measured by Freedom House's 'Freedom on the Net' reports, has declined for 14 consecutive years as of the 2024 report. The 2024 report (published October 2024) specifically stated that protections diminished in 27 countries and improved in 18. Similarly, the 2023 report noted 29 countries deteriorated and 20 registered gains, and previous reports explicitly marked overall declines. Thus, the condition explicitly holds for the 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024 editions. 

The question resolves positively if this trend continues up to the 2025 report inclusive. Since the 2024 report already confirmed the decline through its coverage period, only the 2025 report remains to be assessed. Given the base rate of 14 consecutive years of decline, and the fact that the broader 'Freedom in the World' 2025 report (released late February 2025) just confirmed a 19th consecutive year of democratic decline (with 60 countries deteriorating and 34 improving), it is highly probable the 2025 'Freedom on the Net' report will also chronicle a net decline in internet freedom. The community prediction of 90% accurately reflects this single-year continuation probability. I predict 91% in alignment with the robust empirical trend and community consensus.
